channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: How New Zealand Became Global Laboratory for Climate Friendly Cows
item.description: New Zealand’s farms are set to become a real-life laboratory for one of agriculture’s toughest climate challenges — reducing the methane cows and sheep belch.
item.dc:creator: Tracy Withers

item.title: Trump’s Top Air Pollution Regulator Is Resigning From EPA
item.description: The top air pollution regulator at the US Environmental Protection Agency is resigning.
item.dc:creator: Zahra Hirji

item.title: EU Aims to Cut Soy, Oilseed Import Dependency for Livestock
item.description: The European Union aims to increase the amount of protein feed crops, such as soybeans, that are sourced from within the 27-nation bloc, cutting its dependency on top suppliers like the US and Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Max Ramsay

item.title: JPMorgan Relaxes Return-to-Office Rules for London Heat Wave
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has relaxed some of the finance industry’s strictest return-to-office requirements for its London staff, after the UK capital sweltered through record-high temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Nil Codina Martinez, Olivia Rudgard, Claudia Cohen, Arno Schuetze

item.title: Peabody Gets US Grant to Boost Rare Earths at Wyoming Coal Mines
item.description: Peabody Energy Corp. was awarded a federal grant to support its efforts to produce rare earth elements and critical minerals in Wyoming, as the US seeks to strengthen its domestic supply chain.
item.dc:creator: Will Wade

item.title: Europe’s Third Major Heat Wave Raises Grid and Water Risks
item.description: Temperatures are soaring across western Europe as the third major heat wave to hit the region this year fuels wildfire danger, strains water supplies and tests power grids.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz

item.title: Banks Face Regulatory Scrutiny in EU Over Heat Exposure
item.description: Europe’s bank watchdog will look at how exposed lenders are to heat-related risks, as temperature records keep getting broken.
item.dc:creator: Frances Schwartzkopff

item.title: EU Plans Energy Labels for Data Centers in a Push for Efficiency
item.description: The European Union wants to propose a sustainability rating system for data centers to encourage greater energy and resource efficiency as the sector’s rapid expansion strains power grids and the environment.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Krukowska, Gian Volpicelli

item.title: Los Angeles Heat Builds Ahead of Hot Spell Across US Southwest
item.description: Temperatures are set to rise across Los Angeles and parts of the southwestern US later this week.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: London’s Hot Summer Prompts JPMorgan to Ease Strict WFH Rules
item.description: Some of the UK capital’s toughest return-to-office policies have been relaxed amid record temperatures
item.dc:creator: Nil Codina Martinez, Olivia Rudgard, Claudia Cohen, Arno Schuetze, Todd Woody
